prodStmt.producer: Institute of Statistical, Social and Economic Research

rspStmt.AuthEnty: Institute of Statistical, Social and Economic Research

div.p: Message: Invalid argument supplied for foreach()

stdyInfo.abstract: The Ghana Socioeconomic panel household survey is a joint effort between the Economic Growth Center at Yale University and the Institute of Statistical, Social and Economic Research at Legon (Accra, Ghana). The survey is meant to remedy a major constraint on the understanding of development in low-income countries - the absence of detailed, multi-level and long-term scientific data that follows individuals over time and describes both the natural and built environment in which the individuals reside. Most data collection efforts are short-term - carried out a one point in time; are limited in scope - collecting information on only a few aspects of the lives of the persons in the study; and when there are multiple rounds of data collection, individuals who leave the study area are dropped. This latter means that the most mobile people are not included in existing surveys and studies, perhaps substantially biasing inferences about who benefits from and who bears the cost of the development process. The goal of this project, which aims to follow all individuals, or a random subset, over time using a comprehensive set of survey instruments is thus to shed new light on long-run processes of economic development.

sumDscr.geogUnit: The data is at the leve of district and community

stdyInfo.notes:
The Household Questionnaire Part A collected on household members, including information on Non-resident Relatives and Spouses.
The Household Questionnaire Part B collected the following data:
Household assets – Farm assets and financial assets Agricultural production - plot, land tenure, inputs, labor, harvest, crop stores and sales, Non-farm Household Enterprises-types, assets and finance, labor, revenue and expenses, Household health - Insurance, Anthropometry, Immunization, Activities of Daily Living, Miscellaneous Health, Health in the Past 2 Weeks, Health in Last 12 Month, Women’s health - fertility and power relations, Men’s health – reproductive health and power relations, Children’s module-health, Digit Span test, Raven’s Pattern Cognitive Assessment, Psychology and Social Networking – psychology, personality questions, social networking and information seeking, Household Consumption and Expenditure and Housing characteristics

dataColl.dataCollector: Institute of Statistical, Social and Economic Research
dataColl.resInstru: The Household Questionnaire for the survey was in two parts, A and B. Questionnaire Part A collected data on household members and Questionnaire Part B collected data on the household and dwelling.

useStmt.citReq: Institute of Statistical, Social and Economic Research. Ghana Socioeconomic Panel Survey 2018-2019, Wave 3 [dataset]. Version 1. Accra: ISSER [producer], 2021. Accra: Ghana ACEIR Hub [distributor], 2021.
useStmt.conditions: Public access data, available to all

var.name: remnant
var.labl: Whether HH is a remnant (head split off)
var.imputation: Whether HH is a remnant (head split off)
var.security: Whether HH is a remnant (head split off)
var.embargo: Whether HH is a remnant (head split off)
var.respUnit: Whether HH is a remnant (head split off)

catgry.labl: Yes, head surveyed as original HH

var.notes: This variable indicates whether the original Wave 1 or 2 household member declared to be the head split off from the household in Wave 3. If they did, we attempted to survey the split off head. If we were able to successfully track & interview the head, we re-assigned them the original HH ID and designated the remnant household as the split off. However, if we couldn't find the split off head for whatever reason (i.e. they could not be tracked, moved out of country, etc.), then we denote the remnant but do not assign them a split off ID.

catgry.catValu: Caretaker of the land and the crops
Family land so had the right to cultivate on
Given to him to farm on it by the government
Got it by himself and started using it
Has been leased to me for 10 years as I lend money to the owners
Have to pay amount of money and give schnapps to the owner
Have to pay an amount of money and give schnapps to the owner of the land
I just saw it and started farming it
I just saw it and started farming on it
I just saw the land and uprooted the tress on it and started farming on it
I just went n saw it and started farming on it
I jux saw it n started farming on it
It belongs to her late husband
It's for her spouse... Was given to her spouse by his father
Just saw it and started farming on it
No one gave to me. Just went to a virgin land and started cultivating
Portion of own land/ home plot
Purchased half of the plot and rented the other half.
Share cropped for a period of time but now owner
Shared cropped initially, later land was divided and now owed by respondent.
She discovered the land and made it her own ever since. So it became her property.
Taking care of the land for my sister
The Catechist of the Church at one point asked her to farm on it but later she begged to farm on it

The lands are family lands passed on from generations to generations. Awuku took over that portion and as result of old age, passed it on to William( his brother) to add to his portion of land to cultivate.
Took part of my land for the garden
Was use for collateral by someone else
After working with the owner of the land he decided to allocate some free of charge to pay of my good work
Allocated community vast land used by me
Catechist of the Global Evangelical Church.
Given to him by the forestry commission
It's family land. Anyone who wants to farm it can.
Late husband farm, he inherited from his father
This particular land belong to the school to which he teaches. So land was begged for from school Authorities.
We don't buy land in this community, u can farm at any place of your choice.

catgry.catValu: Although paid for, land will be given to another if left unused
Any member of my family can start working on the land
Children will farm or will take over
Going against the rules for the plot
I don't have any other plot of land and therefore can't leave fallow.
If he pay the land due to the chief, he can leave the land fallow for as long as he wants. However if he does not pay the land's rent he would be mad eto leave the land.
If i Can't farm any longer and my children decides not to farm on it
If i stopped farming there, someone else may come and farm there.
It a family land that might soon be divided
It for someone so the owner might take it
It wiil never be lost but passed on to the next generation.
Land is for the church so they can claim at any time
Once the owner pays the mortgage I will release the land
Other family members will take it
Somebody else would beg for it
The forestry commission can take the land
The land is a family land. Anyone Can farm on it but with permission from the caretakers.
The land owner will take it back
The landlord has the right to demand for it
The owner will asked for it
They will think you might not need it
Unless I am evicted by Government Officials
Unless I leave the land uncultivated for more than 7 years
Unless am not more a life.
Unless am old and can't no longer farm.
Unless i decides not to farm and my children do same.
Unless i decides not to farm there again.
Unless the owner asks for it
Unless the owner asks me for the land back
Until the church decided to use it.
When I Stopped farming and if non of my children also want to farm
When am old and can no longer farm.
When crops on farm become extinct
When he decides not to farm on it again
When he stops farming on it and hand it over t9 someone
When i decides to give it to someone else.
When you go against the rules regarding the plot
Would not get a different land to farm.

catgry.labl: Breach of contract by former owner
Breach of contract by potential buyer

catgry.catValu: Chief wants to sell and give them a portion they think is not enogh
Could not farm a particular season and when the time was due to farm, the one who was using the land wanted to refuse to handover to him
Former wife wanted custody of land
Land was given to someone else to farm but the land owner collected and gave to me to cultivate
Someone else wanted to also farm on it
The land owner at times want to divide the land and redistribute it to other people.
Use of same land that i have been cultivating for some years now

catgry.catValu: A Maize processing machine was used
Cassava farm was sold to a buyer to harvest
Daughter husband people came to raise the yam mouse
Hired Travelling Tractor Man for Dehusking of maize Cob at farm and transported them home
Hired Tricycle to transport goods from the farm
Martin said during the harvesting stage he donated this particular farm produce to some needy people. So he has no idea how many bags they had.
Person to whom land has been rented to
Person who shares farm produce with [PII]
Sent buyers to the field to harvest portion paid for
Sold the cassava and the market women harvested it themselves and one rope was sold for GhÃ‚Â¢100.
Amount paid for clearing covers the plowing as well
Amount paid for clearing covers the plowing too
Amount paid for weeding covers the plowing
Did not pay at the time of planting. She paid them with some of the harvested Maize.
After application of the fertilizer on the whole farm
After completing the weeding is done
After completion of the clearing and land preparation
After spraying the entire number of ropes given.
After the chemical application was done
After the harvest of the cocoa
After the spraying was done on the land
After the whole weeding is done
At the end of clearing and preparing the land
At the end of the chemical application
At the end of weeding period
At the end of weeding the plot
For the entire work on the farm
He pays them through the proceed from the cocoa
Sprayed according to the number of times the spraying machine was filled and used(2*). So 2*3(price of 1) = 6ghC
1 basket of harvested crop to each person
Any of the planted trees. Banana or Plantain
Depending on how each person works, they receive items worth 50 - 100Gh.
Depending on how each person works, they receive items worth 50 and 100Gh each
four bowls of maize to each hired larbour
